summary: The test_tcl module failed with 2 test failures during a Windows CPython test run. The build phase completed successfully, but test execution revealed issues in the Tcl/Tk integration tests. The failures were reproducible when the test was re-run.
root_cause: Two assertions in test.test_tcl.TclTest failed, but the specific failure details are not included in the provided log context. The test module passed 9 individual tests but encountered 2 failures and 3 skipped tests.

evidence.reason: Direct indication that test_tcl module encountered 2 test failures during execution
Test was re-run and the same test_tcl failure occurred again, indicating a consistent, reproducible failure
Out of 505 test files, only 1 failed (test_tcl), confirming isolated test failure
Tests ran with the compiled Windows 64-bit debug Python executable, confirming build phase succeeded

suggested_fix: Review the full test output logs for test_tcl to see the specific assertion errors and stack traces. The failure may be related to Tcl/Tk integration on Windows with the switch-case build configuration. Run the test locally to reproduce the 2 failures and inspect the detailed error messages.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
